sentences.zh: 傳曰亡懷土，何必思故鄉！

translations.literal: The tradition says: do not cling to native soil—why must one think on the old village!
translations.idiomatic: The classic says, "A gentleman does not pine for native soil"—why insist on going home?

sentences.zh: 生其強飯慎疾以自輔。」

translations.literal: You, force eating and be careful of illness to assist yourself."
translations.idiomatic: Eat well, guard your health, and stay at my side."

sentences.zh: 後月餘，以禹為長信少府。

translations.literal: After a bit more than a month, by Yu was made Superintendent of the Household to the Empress Dowager of Changxin.
translations.idiomatic: A month later he named Gong Yu superintendent of the household to the Grand Empress Dowager at Changxin Palace.

sentences.zh: 會御史大夫陳萬年卒，禹代為御史大夫，列於三公。

translations.literal: It happened Imperial Counselor Chen Wannian died; Yu replaced him as imperial counselor, standing among the Three Dukes.
translations.idiomatic: When Imperial Counselor Chen Wannian died, Gong Yu succeeded him and took a seat among the Three Dukes.

sentences.zh: 自禹在位，數言得失，書數十上。

translations.literal: From the time Yu held office, several times spoke of gain and loss; memorials several tens submitted.
translations.idiomatic: As grand counselor he repeatedly debated policy, filing dozens of memorials.

sentences.zh: 禹以為古民亡賦算口錢，起武帝征伐四夷，重賦於民，民產子三歲則出口錢，故民重困，至於生子輒殺，甚可悲痛。

translations.literal: Yu thought that in antiquity the people had no poll tax or mouth cash; it arose when Emperor Wu campaigned against the four barbarians, heavily taxed the people; the people's producing sons at three years then issued mouth cash; therefore the people were heavily distressed, reaching the point of at birth killing sons—very much to be mourned.
translations.idiomatic: Gong Yu noted that antiquity knew no poll tax until Emperor Wu's foreign wars drove the treasury to levy a "mouth cash" on infants as young as three; families were crushed until many killed newborns—a horror that still haunts the realm.

sentences.zh: 宜令兒七歲去齒乃出口錢，年二十乃算。

translations.literal: It is fitting to order children at seven years when teeth fall then to issue mouth cash; at twenty years then to be assessed.
translations.idiomatic: Levy mouth cash only after the seventh year, when milk teeth fall, and full poll tax only at twenty.

sentences.zh: 又言古者不以金錢為幣，專意於農，故一夫不耕，必有受其飢者。

translations.literal: Again he said in antiquity they did not use gold and money as currency; they concentrated intent on farming; therefore if one husband did not plow, there must be those who received hunger from it.
translations.idiomatic: Antiquity did not make coin the heart of commerce; agriculture came first, so if one farmer idled, others went hungry.

sentences.zh: 今漢家鑄錢，及諸鐵官皆置吏卒徒，攻山取銅鐵，一歲功十萬人已上，中農食七人，是七十萬人常受其飢也。

translations.literal: Now the Han house casts cash, and all the iron offices all set clerks, soldiers, and convicts, attacking mountains to take copper and iron—one year's labor more than a hundred thousand persons; middle farmers feed seven persons—this is seven hundred thousand persons constantly receiving hunger from it.
translations.idiomatic: Today mints and iron monopolies deploy clerks, troops, and convicts to strip mountains of ore—well over a hundred thousand laborers a year. Seven commoners eat on one middle farmer's yield; that means seven hundred thousand mouths starve for every mint we run.

sentences.zh: 鑿地數百丈，銷陰氣之精，地臧空虛，不能含氣出雲，斬伐林木亡有時禁，水旱之災未必不繇此也。

translations.literal: Digging earth several hundred zhang, melting the essence of yin vapor, the earth treasury empty and void, unable to contain vapor and emit clouds; cutting and felling forest trees without timely prohibition—water and drought disasters not necessarily not arising from this.
translations.idiomatic: Pits hundreds of feet deep drain the land's vital energies; the hollowed earth can no longer hold moisture or send up rain clouds; forests are stripped without seasonal restraint—flood and drought may well follow.

sentences.zh: 自五銖錢起已來七十餘年，民坐盜鑄錢被刑者眾，富人積錢滿室，猶亡厭足。

translations.literal: From the time the five-zhu cash arose, more than seventy years, the people sitting for stealing casting cash and receiving punishment are many; rich people accumulating cash filling the chamber still are not satisfied.
translations.idiomatic: Seventy years after the five-zhu coin, jails still swarm with counterfeiters while the rich fill vaults and still grasp for more.

sentences.zh: 民心搖動，商賈求利，東西南北各用智巧，好衣美食，歲有十二之利，而不出租稅。

translations.literal: The people's hearts shake and move; merchants seek profit east, west, south, north each using wit and craft; fine clothes and good food, yearly having twelve percent profit, yet do not pay rent and tax.
translations.idiomatic: Popular mind chases coin; merchants roam the empire with clever schemes, live in silks and delicacies, reap twelve percent a year, and pay no land tax.

sentences.zh: 農夫父子暴露中野，不避寒暑，捽屮杷土，手足胼胝，已奉穀租，又出槁稅，鄉部私求，不可勝供。

translations.literal: Farmer fathers and sons are exposed in the central wilds, not avoiding cold and heat; grasping grass and raking earth, hands and feet calloused; already presenting grain rent, again issuing straw tax, village and department private demands—not able to be supplied in full.
translations.idiomatic: Farmers and sons toil in the open, heedless of weather, hands horny from the hoe; after grain rent and fodder levies, petty clerks still squeeze them for more than flesh can yield.

sentences.zh: 故民棄本逐末，耕者不能半。

translations.literal: Therefore the people abandon root and chase tips; plowers are not able to be half.
translations.idiomatic: So the people abandon the plow for trade; fewer than half the fields stay under cultivation.

sentences.zh: 貧民雖賜之田，猶賤賣以賈，窮則起為盜賊。

translations.literal: Poor people although fields are bestowed on them, still cheaply sell them to merchants; in extremity then rise and become bandits.
translations.idiomatic: Even when the poor receive land grants, they sell cheap to speculators; driven to desperation they turn bandit.

sentences.zh: 何者？

translations.literal: What is the reason?
translations.idiomatic: Why?

sentences.zh: 末利深而惑於錢也。

translations.literal: Tip profit is deep and they are deluded by cash.
translations.idiomatic: Because trade pays and money dazzles them.

sentences.zh: 是以姦邪不可禁，其原皆起於錢也。

translations.literal: Therefore treacherous evil cannot be forbidden; its origins all arise from cash.
translations.idiomatic: Vice spreads from the same root—the obsession with coin.

sentences.zh: 疾其末者絕其本，宜罷採珠玉金銀鑄錢之官，亡復以為幣。

translations.literal: Hating its tips, cut off its root; it is fitting to abolish offices that gather pearls, jade, gold, and silver and cast cash; do not again use them as currency.
translations.idiomatic: Strike the evil at its source: close the mints and the pearl-and-gold workshops and stop treating metal as money.

sentences.zh: 市井勿得販賣，除其租銖之律，租稅祿賜皆以布帛及穀。

translations.literal: Markets and wards must not obtain selling; remove the statutes on rent and grain-fine; rent, tax, salary, and bestowals all use cloth, silk, and grain.
translations.idiomatic: Forbid coin in the markets, repeal the petty surcharges on grain, and pay taxes, salaries, and imperial gifts in silk and grain alone.

sentences.zh: 使百姓壹歸於農，復古道便。

translations.literal: Cause the hundred surnames uniformly to return to farming; restore the ancient way—convenient.
translations.idiomatic: Then the people will return to the soil and the old order can be restored with ease.

sentences.zh: 又言諸離宮及長樂宮衛可減其太半，以寬繇役。

translations.literal: Again he said the various detached palaces and guards of Changle Palace can be reduced by more than half, thereby to widen corvée labor.
translations.idiomatic: He urged cutting guards on detached palaces and Changle Palace by more than half to ease corvée.

sentences.zh: 又諸官奴婢十萬餘人戲遊亡事，稅良民以給之，歲費五六鉅萬，宜免為庶人，廩食，令代關東戍卒，乘北邊亭塞候望。

translations.literal: Again the various offices' slave girls and servants more than a hundred thousand persons play and roam without work; taxing good people to supply them, yearly expense five or six tens of millions—it is fitting to free them as commoners, granary feed them, order them to replace the east-of-the-pass garrison soldiers, ride the northern border pavilions and passes and watch.
translations.idiomatic: More than a hundred thousand palace slaves idle at public expense—fifty or sixty million a year. Free them as commoners, feed them from state granaries, and use them to relieve eastern garrisons on the northern frontier.

sentences.zh: 又欲令近臣自諸曹侍中以上，家亡得私販賣，與民爭利，犯者輒免官削爵，不得仕宦。

translations.literal: Again he wished to order close ministers from the various bureaus and attendants-in-ordinary upward, households must not obtain private selling, contending profit with the people; offenders then remove office and strip rank, not able to serve in office.
translations.idiomatic: He would forbid any intimate minister from the attendants-in-ordinary up to trade on the side; violators would be dismissed, stripped of rank, and barred forever from office.

sentences.zh: 禹又言：

translations.literal: Yu again said:
translations.idiomatic: Gong Yu added:

sentences.zh: 孝文皇帝時，貴廉絜，賤貪汙，賈人贅婿及吏坐贓者皆禁錮不得為吏，賞善罰惡，不阿親戚，罪白者伏其誅，疑者以與民，亡贖罪之法，故令行禁止，海內大化，天下斷獄四百，與刑錯亡異。

translations.literal: In the time of Emperor Wen, they honored purity and despised greed and filth; merchants, sons-in-law who live with wives' families, and clerks sitting for embezzlement were all locked and barred from becoming clerks; they rewarded good and punished evil, did not flatter kin; where guilt was clear they submitted to execution, the doubtful they gave to the people; there was no law of ransom for crimes—therefore orders moved and prohibitions stood, within the seas great transformation, all under Heaven judged cases four hundred—no different from setting punishments aside.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Wen probity was honored and graft despised: merchants, uxorilocal sons-in-law, and corrupt officials were barred from service; rewards and punishments ignored connections; clear guilt meant death, doubtful cases went to the people; there was no buying off sentences—so commands held, the realm reformed, and the empire heard barely four hundred capital cases a year, as if the rack stood idle.

sentences.zh: 武帝始臨天下，尊賢用士，闢地廣境數千里，自見功大威行，遂從耆欲，用度不足，乃行壹切之變，使犯法者贖罪，入穀者補吏，是以天下奢侈，官亂民貧，盜賊並起，亡命者眾。

translations.literal: When Emperor Wu first faced all under Heaven, he honored the worthy and used knights, opened land and broadened territory several thousand li; himself seeing merit great and awesomeness exercised, then followed old desire; expenses were not enough, then carried out all-at-once changes, causing lawbreakers to ransom crimes, those who presented grain to supplement clerks—therefore all under Heaven was extravagant, offices chaotic and people poor, bandits arose together, fugitives were many.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wu began by honoring talent and pushing the borders thousands of li; intoxicated by his own power, he chased every appetite until the treasury failed; then he sold pardons and sold offices—luxury spread, government rotted, the people grew poor, banditry exploded, and fugitives filled the hills.

sentences.zh: 郡國恐伏其誅，則擇便巧史書習於計簿能欺上府者，以為右職；

translations.literal: Commanderies and states fearing submitting to their execution, then chose handy, clever clerks skilled in account books able to deceive the superior office, and made them right-hand posts;
translations.idiomatic: Terrified of imperial punishment, local officials promoted slick clerks who could cook the books;

sentences.zh: 姦軌不勝，則取勇猛能操切百姓者，以苛暴威服下者，使居大位。

translations.literal: when treachery and deviance could not be overcome, then took fierce and brave ones able to harshly cut the hundred surnames, thereby violently to awe those below, and caused them to occupy great position.
translations.idiomatic: when fraud still spread, they elevated brutal men who cowed the commoners by terror.

sentences.zh: 故亡義而有財者顯於世，欺謾而善書者尊於朝，誖逆而勇猛者貴於官。

translations.literal: Therefore those without right but having wealth were displayed in the age; those deceitful and good at writing were honored at court; those perverse and fierce and brave were prized in office.
translations.idiomatic: So the shameless rich flaunted themselves, smooth writers lorded it at court, and violent men won high posts.

sentences.zh: 故俗皆曰：「何以孝弟為？

translations.literal: Therefore custom all said: "Why take filial piety and fraternal duty?"
translations.idiomatic: Hence the proverb: "Who needs filial sons when cash brings glory?"

sentences.zh: 財多而光榮。

translations.literal: Much wealth and then glory and honor.
translations.idiomatic: "Heaps of gold mean honor enough."

sentences.zh: 何以禮義為？

translations.literal: Why take ritual and right?"
translations.idiomatic: "Who needs ritual when ledgers win office?"

sentences.zh: 史書而仕宦。

translations.literal: Be skilled at writing and serve in office.
translations.idiomatic: "Scratch a fair hand and you earn a cap."

sentences.zh: 何以謹慎為？

translations.literal: Why take cautious carefulness?"
translations.idiomatic: "Who wants caution when—"

sentences.zh: 勇猛而臨官。」

translations.literal: Fierce and brave and face office."
translations.idiomatic: "—ferocity buys a magistrate's seat?"

sentences.zh: 故黥劓而髡鉗者猶復攘臂為政於世，行雖犬彘，家富勢足，目指氣使，是為賢耳。

translations.literal: Therefore those tattooed, nosed, shaved, and manacled still again bare arms and conduct government in the age; conduct although dog and pig, household wealth and power sufficient, eye pointing and vapor commanding—this is called worthy.
translations.idiomatic: Branded felons still wave their arms in power; men viler than dogs pass for worthies if their coffers are full.

sentences.zh: 故謂居官而置富者為雄桀，處姦而得利者為壯士，兄勸其弟，父勉其子，俗之壞敗，乃至於是！

translations.literal: Therefore they call those who in office and establish wealth heroic and outstanding; those who dwell in treachery and obtain profit strong knights; elder brothers urge younger brothers, fathers exhort sons—custom's ruin reaches to this!
translations.idiomatic: Office is praised as heroism if it makes you rich; crime is hailed as courage if it pays; brothers urge brothers and fathers sons toward the same vice—morals have sunk this low!

sentences.zh: 察其所以然者，皆以犯法得贖罪，求士不得真賢，相守崇財利，誅不行之所致也。

translations.literal: Examining that whereby it was so—all arose from obtaining ransom for crimes by breaking the law, seeking knights without obtaining true worthies, mutual guarding and honoring wealth and profit, punishments not being carried out.
translations.idiomatic: The cause is plain: ransom laws, false appointments, officials who worship profit, and punishments that never fall on the powerful.

sentences.zh: 今欲興至治，致太平，宜除贖罪之法。

translations.literal: Now wishing to raise utmost order and bring about great peace, it is fitting to remove the law of ransom for crimes.
translations.idiomatic: To seek true peace, abolish the sale of pardons.

sentences.zh: 相守選舉不以實，及有臧者，輒行其誅，亡但免官，則爭盡力為善，貴孝弟，賤賈人，進真賢，舉實廉，而天下治矣。

translations.literal: Mutual guarding in selection and recommendation not according to fact, and when there are those with embezzlement, then carry out their execution—not only remove office—then they will contend to exhaust strength in doing good, honor filial piety and fraternal duty, despise merchants, advance true worthies, raise true purity, and all under Heaven will be ordered.
translations.idiomatic: When recommendations lie, or when embezzlers are caught, execute them—do not merely dismiss—and the empire will struggle toward virtue again: filial duty honored, merchants scorned, true men advanced, and order restored.

sentences.zh: 孔子，匹夫之人耳，以樂道正身不解之故，四海之內，天下之君，微孔子之言亡所折中。

translations.literal: Confucius was a mere common man; because of delighting in the Way and correcting the body without slackening, within the four seas, lords of all under Heaven, without Confucius's words had nowhere to decide the mean.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius was only a commoner, yet because he never tired of moral self-cultivation, every ruler under Heaven needs his words to find the mean.

sentences.zh: 況乎以漢地之廣，陛下之德，處南面之尊，秉萬乘之權，因天地之助，其於變世易俗，調和陰陽，陶冶萬物，化正天下，易於決流抑隊。

translations.literal: How much more with the breadth of Han territory, Your Majesty's virtue, occupying the honor of facing south, grasping the authority of ten thousand chariots, relying on Heaven and Earth's aid—as to changing the age and altering custom, harmonizing yin and yang, molding and smelting the myriad things, transforming and correcting all under Heaven—it is easier than blocking a rushing current and catching a falling pillar.
translations.idiomatic: With Han's breadth, your virtue, the Son of Heaven's station, the weight of ten thousand chariots, and Heaven and earth behind you, remaking the age, tuning yin and yang, and casting the people in a better mold ought to be easier than turning back a flood or propping up a tottering beam.

sentences.zh: 自成康以來，幾且千歲，欲為治者甚眾，然而太平不復興者，何也？

translations.literal: From Cheng and Kang down, nearly a thousand years; those who wished to bring about order have been very many, yet great peace has not again arisen—why?
translations.idiomatic: Since the age of Cheng and Kang nearly a thousand years have passed; many have claimed to seek order, yet great peace never returned—why?

sentences.zh: 以其舍法度而任私意，奢侈行而仁義廢也。

translations.literal: Because they discarded law and measurement and trusted private intent; extravagance proceeded and benevolence and right were abandoned.
translations.idiomatic: Because each ruler abandoned law for whim, chased luxury, and let benevolence and duty decay.

sentences.zh: 陛下誠深念高祖之苦，醇法太宗之治，正己以先下，選賢以自輔，開進忠正，致誅姦臣，遠放諂佞，放出園陵之女，罷倡樂，絕鄭聲，去甲乙之帳，退偽薄之物，修節儉之化，驅天下之民皆歸於農，如此不解，則三王可侔，五帝可及。

translations.literal: If Your Majesty sincerely deeply thinks on Gaozu's hardships, takes as pure law the governance of the Grand Exemplar (Taizong), corrects self to lead those below, selects the worthy to assist self, opens and advances loyal and upright, brings execution on treacherous ministers, banishes far flattery and fawning, releases out the women of garden tombs, stops singing-girls and music, cuts off Zheng sounds, removes curtains of jia and yi, retires false and shallow things, cultivates restraint and frugality's transformation, drives all under Heaven's people all to return to farming—like this without slackening—then the Three Kings can be matched, the Five Thearchs can be reached.
translations.idiomatic: If you will remember Gaozu's hardships, model yourself soberly on Emperor Wen, set the example, appoint worthies, welcome honest counsel, execute corrupt ministers, drive away sycophants, free the tomb attendants, silence lewd music, strip the layered gauze hangings, cast off sham finery, teach thrift, and steer every subject back to the plow without flagging—you could stand beside the Three Kings and tread the path of the Five Thearchs.

sentences.zh: 唯陛下留意省察，天下幸甚。

translations.literal: Only may Your Majesty keep mind and examine; all under Heaven will be greatly fortunate.
translations.idiomatic: I beg you to weigh these words—for the empire's sake as well as your own.

sentences.zh: 天子下其議，令民產子七歲乃出口錢，自此始。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven sent down his discussion, ordered the people's producing sons at seven years then to issue mouth cash—from this it began.
translations.idiomatic: The court adopted part of his program: the poll on infants began at seven years from that day forward.

sentences.zh: 又罷上林宮館希幸御者，及省建章、甘泉宮衛卒，減諸侯王廟衛卒省其半。

translations.literal: Again abolished Shanglin palace lodges rarely favored by the imperial progress, and saved Jianzhang and Sweet Spring Palace guard soldiers, reduced the feudal kings' temple guard soldiers and saved half of them.
translations.idiomatic: He also closed rarely visited lodges in the Shanglin park, cut guards on Jianzhang and Sweet Spring palaces, and halved the garrisons at imperial princes' shrines.

sentences.zh: 餘雖未盡從，然嘉其質直之意。

translations.literal: The rest although not entirely followed, yet praised his quality of straightforward intent.
translations.idiomatic: The throne did not grant every request but honored his blunt honesty.

sentences.zh: 禹又奏欲罷郡國廟，定漢宗廟迭毀之禮，皆未施行。

translations.literal: Yu again submitted wishing to abolish commandery and state temples, fix Han ancestral temple successive-destruction rites—all were not carried out.
translations.idiomatic: He also urged abolishing local shrines to Han emperors and fixing the rotation of remote versus close ancestral temples—measures left unenacted for the moment.

sentences.zh: 為御史大夫數月卒，天子賜錢百萬，以其子為郎，官至東郡都尉。

translations.literal: Serving as imperial counselor several months he died; the Son of Heaven bestowed cash a million, made his son gentleman, rank reaching metropolitan commandant of the eastern commandery.
translations.idiomatic: He died a few months after becoming imperial counselor; the emperor gave his family a million cash, appointed his son a gentleman, who rose eventually to metropolitan commandant of the eastern commandery.

sentences.zh: 禹卒後，上追思其議，竟下詔罷郡國廟，定迭毀之禮。

translations.literal: After Yu died, the emperor pursued thinking on his discussions, finally issued an edict abolishing commandery and state temples, fixing successive-destruction rites.
translations.idiomatic: After Gong Yu's death the emperor reflected on his memorials and at last ordered the local imperial shrines closed and the ancestral rotation rites codified.

sentences.zh: 語在韋玄成傳。

translations.literal: The words are in the biography of Wei Xuancheng.
translations.idiomatic: The details are recorded in the biography of Wei Xuancheng.

sentences.zh: 兩龔皆楚人也，勝字君賓，舍字君倩。

translations.literal: Both Gongs were men of Chu; Sheng courtesy name Junbin, She courtesy name Junqian.
translations.idiomatic: The two Gongs, natives of Chu, were Gong Sheng, style Junbin, and Gong She, style Junqian.

sentences.zh: 二人相友，並著名節，故世謂之楚兩龔。

translations.literal: The two men were mutually friends, and together were renowned for integrity; therefore the age calls them the two Gongs of Chu.
translations.idiomatic: They were fast friends and equally famed for principle, hence the phrase "the two Gongs of Chu."

sentences.zh: 少皆好學明經，勝為郡吏，舍不仕。

translations.literal: In youth both loved learning and illumined the classics; Sheng became a commandery clerk, She did not serve.
translations.idiomatic: Both studied the classics in youth; Gong Sheng took a commandery post while Gong She refused office.

sentences.zh: 久之，楚王入朝，聞舍高明，聘舍為常侍，不得已隨王，歸國固辭，願卒學，復至長安。

translations.literal: After a long time the king of Chu entered court, heard She was lofty and bright, hired She as regular attendant; unavoidably following the king, returning to the state firmly declined, wished to finish study, again arrived at Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: When the king of Chu later visited the capital, he heard of Gong She's reputation and made him a regular attendant; Gong She had no choice but to follow the king home, then resigned firmly, returned to Chang'an to complete his studies.

sentences.zh: 而勝為郡吏，三舉孝廉，以王國人不得宿衛補吏。

translations.literal: And Sheng was a commandery clerk, three times recommended filial and incorrupt, because kingdom person could not lodge guard and supplement clerk.
translations.idiomatic: Gong Sheng served as a commandery clerk and thrice earned filial-and-incorrupt recommendation, but as a subject of a kingdom he could not hold capital guard posts.

sentences.zh: 再為尉，壹為丞，勝輒至官乃去。

translations.literal: Twice was captain, once was aide; Sheng then reached office and then departed.
translations.idiomatic: Twice he was a district captain and once an aide—each time he quit as soon as he took the post.

sentences.zh: 州舉茂材，為重泉令，病去官。

translations.literal: The province raised him as abundant talent; he became magistrate of Chongquan, by illness left office.
translations.idiomatic: The province nominated him as "abundant talent," and he became magistrate of Chongquan until illness forced him out.

sentences.zh: 大司空何武、執金吾閻崇薦勝，哀帝自為定陶王固已聞其名，徵為諫大夫。

translations.literal: Grand Minister of Works He Wu and Bearer of the Mace Yan Chong recommended Sheng; Emperor Ai from the time he was king of Dingtao firmly had heard his name, summoned him to be remonstrance grandee.
translations.idiomatic: He Wu and Yan Chong recommended Gong Sheng; Emperor Ai, who had known his name even as prince of Dingtao, summoned him as remonstrance grandee.

sentences.zh: 引見，勝薦龔舍及亢父甯壽、濟陰侯嘉，有詔皆徵。

translations.literal: Led to audience, Sheng recommended Gong She and Ning Shou of Kangfu, Hou Jia of Jiyin; there was an edict summoning all.
translations.idiomatic: At audience Gong Sheng recommended Gong She, Ning Shou of Kangfu, and Hou Jia of Jiyin; the throne summoned them all.

sentences.zh: 勝曰：「竊見國家徵醫巫，常為駕，徵賢者宜駕。」

translations.literal: Sheng said: "Your servant privately sees the state when summoning doctors and shamans always provides chariots; summoning the worthy it is fitting to provide chariots."
translations.idiomatic: Gong Sheng observed, "When the court calls physicians or shamans it sends carriages; it should do no less for worthies."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「大夫乘私車來邪？」

translations.literal: The emperor said: "Grandee, you came by private carriage, did you?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked, "Did you arrive in your own carriage, sir?"

sentences.zh: 勝曰：「唯唯。」

translations.literal: Sheng said: "Yes, yes."
translations.idiomatic: Gong Sheng answered, "I did."

sentences.zh: 有詔為駕。

translations.literal: There was an edict to provide a carriage.
translations.idiomatic: An edict at once ordered a state carriage for him.

sentences.zh: 龔舍、侯嘉至，皆為諫大夫。

translations.literal: Gong She and Hou Jia arrived; both became remonstrance grandees.
translations.idiomatic: Gong She and Hou Jia came to the capital and were both named remonstrance grandees.

sentences.zh: 甯壽稱疾不至。

translations.literal: Ning Shou claimed illness and did not arrive.
translations.idiomatic: Ning Shou pleaded illness and stayed away.

sentences.zh: 勝居諫官，數上書求見，言百姓貧，盜賊多，吏不良，風俗薄，災異數見，不可不憂。

translations.literal: Sheng dwelt as remonstrance official, several times submitted memorials seeking audience, speaking that the hundred surnames were poor, bandits many, clerks not good, custom thin, disasters and anomalies several times appeared—cannot but be worried.
translations.idiomatic: As remonstrator Gong Sheng repeatedly sought audience to warn that the people were destitute, banditry rife, officials corrupt, morals thin, and omens frequent—ills that brooked no complacency.

sentences.zh: 制度泰奢，刑罰泰深，賦斂泰重，宜以儉約先下。

translations.literal: Institutions too extravagant, punishments too deep, levies too heavy—it is fitting to use frugality and restraint to lead those below.
translations.idiomatic: Institutions had grown extravagant, penalties harsh, and taxes crushing; the court should lead by austerity.

sentences.zh: 其言祖述王吉、貢禹之意。

translations.literal: His words largely followed the ideas of Wang Ji and Gong Yu.
translations.idiomatic: His arguments echoed Wang Ji and Gong Yu.

sentences.zh: 為大夫二歲餘，遷丞相司直，徙光祿大夫，守右扶風。

translations.literal: Serving as grandee a bit more than two years, was transferred Director of Integrity for the Chancellor, moved to Grand Counselor of the Household, guarded Right Fufeng.
translations.idiomatic: After two years as grandee he became the chancellor's director of integrity, then grand counselor of the household, then acting governor of Right Fufeng.

sentences.zh: 數月，上知勝非撥煩吏，乃復還勝光祿大夫諸吏給事中。

translations.literal: Several months, the emperor knew Sheng was not a clerk for sorting tangles, then again returned Sheng to Grand Counselor of the Household, various clerks, attendant within.
translations.idiomatic: Within months the emperor saw he was no routine administrator and moved him back to grand counselor of the household with attendant-at-palace duties.

sentences.zh: 勝言董賢亂制度，繇是逆上指。

translations.literal: Sheng spoke that Dong Xian disordered institutions; thereby he opposed the emperor's intent.
translations.idiomatic: He denounced Dong Xian for corrupting government and thus crossed the emperor's will.

sentences.zh: 後歲餘，丞相王嘉上書薦故廷尉梁相等，尚書劾奏嘉「言事恣意，迷國罔上，不道。」

translations.literal: After more than a year Chancellor Wang Jia submitted a memorial recommending former Commandant of Justice Liang Xiang and others; the Secretariat impeached Jia saying "his words on affairs follow his whim, deludes the state and deceives the superior—not the Way."
translations.idiomatic: A year later Chancellor Wang Jia recommended the former commandant of justice Liang Xiang; the secretariat impeached him for "reckless counsel that misleads the state and deceives the throne—conduct incompatible with the Way."

sentences.zh: 下將軍中朝者議，左將軍公孫祿、司隸鮑宣、光祿大夫孔光等十四人皆以為嘉應迷國不道法。

translations.literal: It was sent down to the generals and middle-court men to discuss; Left General Gongsun Lu, Metropolitan Superintendent Bao Xuan, Grand Counselor of the Household Kong Guang and fourteen others all held that Jia ought to be guilty of deluding the state by the Way of law.
translations.idiomatic: The case went to the generals and inner court: Gongsun Lu, Bao Xuan, Kong Guang, and fourteen others voted that Wang Jia had indeed misled the state.

sentences.zh: 勝獨書議曰：「嘉資性邪僻，所舉多貪殘吏。

translations.literal: Sheng alone wrote the discussion saying: "Jia's disposition is wicked and perverse; those he raised are mostly greedy and cruel clerks.
translations.idiomatic: Gong Sheng alone filed a dissent: "Wang Jia's character is twisted and the men he promotes are mostly cruel grafters.

sentences.zh: 位列三公，陰陽不和，諸事並廢，咎皆繇嘉，迷國不疑，今舉相等，過微薄。」

translations.literal: Standing in rank among the Three Dukes, yin and yang not harmonious, the various affairs together abandoned—the fault all arises from Jia; deluding the state is not doubtful. Now raising Xiang and others—the fault is slight and small."
translations.idiomatic: As a minister of state he threw yin and yang out of joint and let every duty lapse—the blame is his. Compared with that, recommending Liang Xiang is a venial slip."

sentences.zh: 日暮議者罷。

translations.literal: At sunset the discussers stopped.
translations.idiomatic: At dusk the session adjourned.

sentences.zh: 明旦復會，左將軍祿問勝：「君議亡所據，今奏當上，宜何從？」

translations.literal: Next dawn they met again; Left General Lu asked Sheng: "Your discussion had no basis; now the memorial ought to go up—what ought one follow?"
translations.idiomatic: Next morning Gongsun Lu asked him, "Your opinion had no textual basis; the draft verdict goes up today—which way will you vote?"

sentences.zh: 勝曰：「將軍以勝議不可者，通劾之。」

translations.literal: Sheng said: "If the general thinks Sheng's discussion unacceptable, then jointly impeach me."
translations.idiomatic: Gong Sheng replied, "If my view offends you, impeach me along with him."

sentences.zh: 博士夏侯常見勝應祿不和，起至勝前謂曰：「宜如奏所言。」

translations.literal: Erudite Xia Hou Chang saw Sheng's response to Lu was not harmonious, rose and went before Sheng saying, "It is fitting to be as the memorial states."
translations.idiomatic: The academician Xia Hou Chang, seeing the clash, stepped up and urged him to sign the majority opinion.

sentences.zh: 勝以手推常曰：「去！」

translations.literal: Sheng with his hand pushed Chang saying, "Go away!"
translations.idiomatic: Gong Sheng shoved him aside and snapped, "Out of my sight!"

sentences.zh: 後數日，復會議可復孝惠、孝景廟不，議者皆曰宜復。

translations.literal: After several days, again met in discussion whether it was permissible to restore the temples of Emperor Hui and Emperor Jing; the discussers all said it was fitting to restore.
translations.idiomatic: A few days later the court reconvened to decide whether to restore the shrines to Emperor Hui and Emperor Jing; the majority voted yes.

sentences.zh: 勝曰：「當如禮。」

translations.literal: Sheng said: "It ought to be as ritual."
translations.idiomatic: Gong Sheng said, "The rites do not permit it."

sentences.zh: 常復謂勝：「禮有變。」

translations.literal: Chang again said to Sheng: "Ritual has changes."
translations.idiomatic: Xia Hou Chang retorted, "Ritual itself allows for change."

sentences.zh: 勝疾言曰：「去！

translations.literal: Sheng sharply said: "Go away!
translations.idiomatic: Gong Sheng snapped, "Be gone!

sentences.zh: 是時之變。」

translations.literal: It is the change of this time."
translations.idiomatic: That is the kind of "change" we have today."

sentences.zh: 常恚，謂勝曰：「我視君何若，君欲小與眾異，外以采名，君乃申徒狄屬耳！」

translations.literal: Chang was resentful, said to Sheng: "I look at you as what sort—you wish slightly to differ from the multitude, outwardly thereby to harvest name—you are of the sort of Shentu Di!"
translations.idiomatic: Chang, furious, sneered, "So you must stand apart from everyone and polish your reputation—you are another Shentu Di!"

sentences.zh: 先是常又為勝道高陵有子殺母者。

translations.literal: Before this Chang again for Sheng spoke that in Gaoling there was a son who killed his mother.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier Chang had told Gong Sheng of a matricide case in Gaoling.

sentences.zh: 勝白之，尚書問：「誰受？」

translations.literal: Sheng reported it; the Secretariat asked: "Who received it?"
translations.idiomatic: Gong Sheng relayed it to the secretariat, which asked who had first brought the report.

sentences.zh: 對曰：「受夏侯常。」

translations.literal: He replied: "Received from Xia Hou Chang."
translations.idiomatic: He answered, "Xia Hou Chang."

sentences.zh: 尚書使勝問常，常連恨勝，即應曰：「聞之白衣，戒君勿言也。

translations.literal: The Secretariat ordered Sheng to ask Chang; Chang continuously hated Sheng, immediately replied: "Heard it from a man in white; he warned you not to speak.
translations.idiomatic: The secretariat told Gong Sheng to confront Chang, who already hated him and answered at once, "I heard it from some commoner who told me to keep silent.

sentences.zh: 奏事不詳，妄作觸罪。」

translations.literal: The memorial on the affair was not detailed; rashly acting touches crime."
translations.idiomatic: Your report was vague; I spoke carelessly and risked a charge."

sentences.zh: 勝窮，亡以對尚書，即自劾奏與常爭言，洿辱朝廷。

translations.literal: Sheng was cornered, had nothing with which to reply to the Secretariat, then personally impeached himself for memorializing that he disputed words with Chang and defiled and insulted the court.
translations.idiomatic: Caught short before the secretariat, Gong Sheng memorialized against himself for brawling with Chang and disgracing the court.

sentences.zh: 事下御史中丞，召詰問，劾奏「勝吏二千石，常位大夫，皆幸得給事中，與論議，不崇禮義，而居公門下相非恨，疾言辯訟，惰謾亡狀，皆不敬。」

translations.literal: The affair was sent down to the assistant imperial secretary; summoned and questioned, impeachment memorial said: "Sheng as clerk at two thousand shi, Chang in rank as grandee—both fortunately obtained attendant within and joined in discussion; they did not honor ritual and right, yet dwelt under the public gate mutually blaming and hating, sharp words and contentious suits, lazy and deceitful without bearing—all disrespectful."
translations.idiomatic: The assistant imperial secretary investigated and charged them: "Gong Sheng held a two-thousand-shi post and Xia Hou Chang the rank of grandee; both enjoyed attendant status at deliberations yet ignored ritual, quarreled at the palace gate, traded insults and suits, and behaved with contempt for all decorum."

sentences.zh: 制曰：「貶秩各一等。」

translations.literal: The edict said: "Demote rank each one grade."
translations.idiomatic: The edict demoted each man one rank.

sentences.zh: 勝謝罪，乞骸骨。

translations.literal: Sheng apologized for crime, begged bones.
translations.idiomatic: Gong Sheng confessed fault and asked to retire.

sentences.zh: 上乃復加賞賜，以子博為侍郎，出勝為渤海太守。

translations.literal: The emperor then again added bestowals, made his son Bo attendant gentleman, sent Sheng out as governor of Bohai.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor instead heaped gifts on him, appointed his son Bo an attendant gentleman, and named him governor of Bohai.

sentences.zh: 勝謝病不任之官，積六月免歸。

translations.literal: Sheng declined illness, did not take up the office; accumulated six months dismissed and returned.
translations.idiomatic: He pleaded illness and never took up the post; after six months he was dismissed and sent home.

sentences.zh: 上復徵為光祿大夫。

translations.literal: The emperor again summoned him to be Grand Counselor of the Household.
translations.idiomatic: The court recalled him as grand counselor of the household.

sentences.zh: 勝常稱疾臥。

translations.literal: Sheng constantly claimed illness and lay down.
translations.idiomatic: He stayed in bed claiming chronic illness.

sentences.zh: 數使子上書乞骸骨，會哀帝崩。

translations.literal: Several times ordered his son to submit memorials begging bones; it happened Emperor Ai died.
translations.idiomatic: He had his son file repeated requests to retire; then Emperor Ai died.

sentences.zh: 初，琅邪邴漢亦以清行徵用，至京兆尹，後為太中大夫。

translations.literal: At first, Bing Han of Langya also by pure conduct was summoned and employed, reaching Metropolitan Governor, afterward became Grand Palace Grandee.
translations.idiomatic: Bing Han of Langya had likewise been summoned for his integrity, rose to metropolitan governor, and later to grand palace grandee.

sentences.zh: 王莽秉政，勝與漢俱乞骸骨。

translations.literal: Wang Mang held power; Sheng and Han together begged bones.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Mang took the reins, Gong Sheng and Bing Han both petitioned to retire.

sentences.zh: 自昭帝時，涿郡韓福以德行徵至京師，賜策書束帛遣歸。

translations.literal: From the time of Emperor Zhao, Han Fu of Zhuo commandery by virtue and conduct was summoned to the capital, was bestowed document on slips and bundled silks and sent back.
translations.idiomatic: Since Emperor Zhao's day, Han Fu of Zhuo had been called to the capital for his virtue, given a written citation and silk, and escorted home in honor.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：「朕閔勞以官職之事，其務修孝弟以教鄉里。

translations.literal: The edict said: "I grieve at laboring you with the affairs of office; you must cultivate filial piety and fraternal duty to teach village and hamlet.
translations.idiomatic: The edict read, "I regret burdening you with office; devote yourself instead to filial piety and brotherly love as a model for your community.

sentences.zh: 行道舍傳舍，縣次具酒肉，食從者及馬。

translations.literal: Walking the road lodge at relay stations; counties in sequence supply wine and meat, feed followers and horses.
translations.idiomatic: On the journey home use the post stations; each county shall furnish wine, meat, and fodder for your escort and horses.

sentences.zh: 長吏以時存問，常以歲八月賜羊一頭，酒二斛。

translations.literal: Senior clerks according to season inquire after you; constantly in the year's eighth month bestow one sheep, two hu of wine.
translations.idiomatic: Local magistrates shall call on you in season and every eighth month send a sheep and two hu of wine.

sentences.zh: 不幸死者，賜複衾一，祠以中牢。」

translations.literal: If unluckily you die, bestow a double coverlet, sacrifice with middle livestock."
translations.idiomatic: If you should die, the state will grant a double shroud and sacrifice with a pig and a sheep."

sentences.zh: 於是王莽依故事，白遣勝、漢。

translations.literal: Thereupon Wang Mang according to old precedent reported sending Sheng and Han.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang followed that precedent in arranging Gong Sheng's and Bing Han's departure.

sentences.zh: 策曰：「惟元始二年六月庚寅，光祿大夫、太中大夫耆艾二人以老病罷。

translations.literal: The document said: "Only in the second year of Yuanshi, sixth month, day gengyin, the Grand Counselor of the Household and Grand Palace Grandee, two old hoary men, by old age and illness are dismissed."
translations.idiomatic: The citation ran: "On the gengyin day of the sixth month in the second year of Yuanshi, the grand counselor of the household and the grand palace grandee, two venerable men, retire for age and infirmity."

sentences.zh: 太皇太后使謁者僕射策詔之曰：蓋聞古者有司年至則致仕，所以恭讓而不盡其力也。

translations.literal: The Grand Empress Dowager ordered the chief usher to document the edict to them, saying: I have heard that in antiquity offices had a year limit then retired, thereby to show respect and yielding and not exhaust their strength.
translations.idiomatic: The grand empress dowager had the chief usher read: "Antiquity set an age for retirement so that ministers might yield their strength in good time.

sentences.zh: 今大夫年至矣，朕愍以官職之事煩大夫，其上子若孫若同產、同產子一人。

translations.literal: Now the grandee's years have reached; I grieve that the affairs of office trouble the grandee—present one son or grandson or full brother or full brother's son."
translations.idiomatic: You have reached that age, and I regret that state business still wearies you—name one son, grandson, full brother, or nephew as your successor."

sentences.zh: 大夫其修身守道，以終高年。

translations.literal: Grandee, cultivate body and guard the Way, thereby to finish high years.
translations.idiomatic: Cultivate yourself in the Way and live out your span in peace.

sentences.zh: 賜帛及行道舍宿，歲時羊酒衣衾，皆如韓福故事。

translations.literal: Bestow silks and walking-road lodging, yearly season sheep, wine, clothes, and coverlets—all according to Han Fu's old precedent.
translations.idiomatic: You shall receive silk, post-station lodging, seasonal sheep, wine, and clothing—exactly as was done for Han Fu.

sentences.zh: 所上子男皆除為郎。」

translations.literal: Those sons presented upward—all remove them to be gentlemen."
translations.idiomatic: The sons you nominate shall all be appointed gentlemen."

sentences.zh: 於是勝、漢遂歸老于鄉里。

translations.literal: Thereupon Sheng and Han finally returned old in village and hamlet.
translations.idiomatic: Gong Sheng and Bing Han thus spent their last years in their home districts.

sentences.zh: 漢兄子曼容亦養志自修，為官不肯過六百石，輒自免去，其名過出於漢。

translations.literal: Han's elder brother's son Manrong also nourished purpose and cultivated himself; in office was not willing to exceed six hundred shi, then himself removed and left; his name surpassed Han's.
translations.idiomatic: Bing Han's nephew Manrong likewise cultivated his character; he refused any salary rank above six hundred shi and quit rather than accept one, and his reputation eclipsed his uncle's.

sentences.zh: 初，龔舍以龔勝薦，徵為諫大夫，病免。

translations.literal: At first, Gong She by Gong Sheng's recommendation was summoned to be remonstrance grandee, by illness dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Gong She, on Gong Sheng's recommendation, had been remonstrance grandee until illness forced him out.

sentences.zh: 復徵為博士，又病去。

translations.literal: Again summoned to be erudite, again by illness departed.
translations.idiomatic: He was recalled as an erudite but again quit on grounds of health.

sentences.zh: 頃之，哀帝遣使者即楚拜舍為太山太守。

translations.literal: Shortly, Emperor Ai sent an envoy to Chu to appoint She as governor of Taishan.
translations.idiomatic: Soon Emperor Ai dispatched an envoy to Chu to name Gong She governor of Taishan.

sentences.zh: 舍家居在武原，使者至縣請舍，欲令至廷拜授印綬。

translations.literal: She's household dwelt in Wuyuan; the envoy arrived at the county and requested She, wishing to order him to come to the courtyard to bow and receive seal and ribbon.
translations.idiomatic: Gong She lived in Wuyuan County; the envoy asked the magistrate to bring him to the yamen for the formal investiture.

sentences.zh: 舍曰：「王者以天下為家，何必縣官？」

translations.literal: She said: "The king who possesses the state takes all under Heaven as home—why must it be the county office?"
translations.idiomatic: Gong She replied, "The Son of Heaven holds the realm as his house; why drag me to a county yamen?"

sentences.zh: 遂於家受詔，便道之官。

translations.literal: Thereupon at home received the edict, by convenient route went to office.
translations.idiomatic: He accepted the edict at home and took the shortest road to his post.

sentences.zh: 既至數月，上書乞骸骨。

translations.literal: Having reached several months, submitted memorial begging bones.
translations.idiomatic: Within months he memorialized to retire.

sentences.zh: 上徵舍，至京兆東湖界，固稱病篤。

translations.literal: The emperor summoned She; reaching the eastern Hu boundary of the Metropolitan District, firmly claimed grave illness.
translations.idiomatic: When the court recalled him, he crossed into the capital district at East Hu and insisted he was dying.

sentences.zh: 天子使使者收印綬，拜舍為光祿大夫。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven ordered an envoy to collect seal and ribbon, appointed She Grand Counselor of the Household.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor sent an envoy to take back his seal and instead named him grand counselor of the household.

sentences.zh: 數賜告，舍終不肯起，乃遣歸。

translations.literal: Several times granted leave; She to the end was not willing to rise, then was sent back.
translations.idiomatic: Repeated sick leave followed; he would not rise from bed, so they let him return home.

sentences.zh: 舍亦通五經，以魯詩教授。

translations.literal: She also penetrated the Five Classics, by the Lu version of the Odes taught.
translations.idiomatic: Gong She too had mastered the Five Classics and taught the Lu recension of the Odes.

sentences.zh: 舍、勝既歸鄉里，郡二千石長吏初到官皆至其家，如師弟子之禮。

translations.literal: She and Sheng having returned to village and hamlet, the commandery's two-thousand-shi senior clerks at first arrival to office all came to their homes, as master and disciple ritual.
translations.idiomatic: After Gong She and Gong Sheng retired, every new governor called on them as on a teacher.

sentences.zh: 舍年六十八，王莽居攝中卒。

translations.literal: She at age sixty-eight, in the middle of Wang Mang's regency, died.
translations.idiomatic: Gong She died at sixty-eight while Wang Mang was regent.

sentences.zh: 莽既篡國，遣五威將帥行天下風俗，將帥親奉羊酒存問勝。

translations.literal: Mang having usurped the state, sent the Five Weights generals to conduct customs of all under Heaven; the generals in person presented sheep and wine and inquired after Sheng.
translations.idiomatic: Once Wang Mang seized the throne he sent the Five Weights commissioners on tour; they called on Gong Sheng with sheep and wine.

sentences.zh: 明年，莽遣使者即拜勝為講學祭酒，勝稱疾不應徵。

translations.literal: The next year, Mang sent an envoy to appoint Sheng as libationer of the lecture hall; Sheng claimed illness and did not respond to the summons.
translations.idiomatic: The following year an envoy tried to name him libationer of the imperial lecture hall; he pleaded illness and ignored the summons.

sentences.zh: 後二年，莽復遣使者奉璽書，太子師友祭酒印綬，安車駟馬迎勝，即拜，秩上卿，先賜六月祿直以辦裝，使者與郡太守、縣長吏、三老官屬、行義諸生千人以上入勝里致詔。

translations.literal: After two years, Mang again sent an envoy presenting the document with imperial seal, seal and ribbon of libationer to the heir apparent's tutor and friend, comfortable carriage and four horses to welcome Sheng, immediate appointment, rank upper minister, first bestowing six months' salary value to prepare luggage—the envoy together with the commandery governor, county senior clerk, elders, and more than a thousand students of conduct and righteousness entered Sheng's hamlet to deliver the edict.
translations.idiomatic: Two years later Wang Mang sent another envoy with the jade-sealed edict, the seals of tutor and friend to the heir apparent, a four-horse carriage, appointment at upper-minister rank, six months' salary in advance for travel expenses, and a procession of the governor, magistrate, village elders, and more than a thousand students to Gong Sheng's lane to read the summons.

sentences.zh: 使者欲令勝起迎，久立門外。

translations.literal: The envoy wished to order Sheng to rise and welcome; long stood outside the gate.
translations.idiomatic: The envoy waited at the gate for Gong Sheng to come out and greet him.

sentences.zh: 勝稱病篤，為床室中戶西南牖下，東首加朝服癴紳。

translations.literal: Sheng claimed grave illness, made a couch in the chamber southwest of the door below the window, east head, put on court dress and tucked sash (jian-shen).
translations.idiomatic: Gong Sheng declared himself dying, had a couch placed beneath the southwest window of his room with his head to the east, and dressed in full court robes with sash properly tucked.

sentences.zh: 使者入戶，西行南面立，致詔付璽書，遷延再拜奉印綬，內安車駟馬，進謂勝曰：「聖朝未嘗忘君，制作未定，待君為政，思聞所欲施行，以安海內。」

translations.literal: The envoy entered the door, went west and stood facing south, delivered the edict and handed the document with seal, delayed and twice bowed presenting seal and ribbon, brought in the comfortable carriage and four horses, advanced and said to Sheng: "The sage court has never forgotten you; institutions are not yet settled—it awaits you to conduct government, wishes to hear what you desire to carry out, thereby to pacify within the seas."
translations.idiomatic: The envoy entered, took station facing south, read the edict and presented the sealed letter, bowed twice, offered the carriage and horses, and said, "The new dynasty has not forgotten you; laws are unfinished—it looks to you for guidance. Tell us what you would enact to settle the realm."

sentences.zh: 勝對曰：「素愚，加以年老被病，命在朝夕，隨使君上道，必死道路，無益萬分。」

translations.literal: Sheng replied saying: "Plain stupid, added to old age and struck by illness, fate lies between morning and evening; following the envoy lord on the road, must die on the road—no benefit in the slightest."
translations.idiomatic: Gong Sheng answered, "I am a dull old man at death's door; if I tried to travel with you I would die on the road and do your cause no good."

sentences.zh: 使者要說，至以印綬就加勝身，勝輒推不受。

translations.literal: The envoy pressed persuasion, reaching the point of placing seal and ribbon on Sheng's body; Sheng then pushed and did not receive.
translations.idiomatic: The envoy pressed him until he tried to drape the seals on Gong Sheng's person; Gong Sheng thrust them away.

sentences.zh: 使者即上言：「方盛夏暑熱，勝病少氣，可須秋涼乃發。」

translations.literal: The envoy immediately submitted words upward: "It is just midsummer heat; Sheng's illness is short of vapor; can wait until autumn cool then set out."
translations.idiomatic: The envoy reported back, "Midsummer heat has left him breathless; let him wait for cool autumn before traveling."

sentences.zh: 有詔許。

translations.literal: There was an edict permitting.
translations.idiomatic: The court assented.

sentences.zh: 使者五日壹與太守俱問起居，為勝兩子及門人高暉等言：「朝廷虛心待君以茅土之封，雖疾病，宜動移至傳舍，示有行意，必為子孫遺大業。」

translations.literal: The envoy every five days once with the governor together asked after rising and resting, for Sheng's two sons and disciples such as Gao Hui spoke: "The court empties its heart awaiting you with fief of reed and earth; although ill, it is fitting to move to the relay station, show intention to travel—surely will for sons and grandsons leave a great enterprise."
translations.idiomatic: Every five days the envoy and governor inquired after his health and told his sons and disciple Gao Hui, "The throne means to enfeoff you; even ill you should move to the post inn to show good will—that will secure great fortune for your heirs."

sentences.zh: 暉等白使者語，勝自知不見聽，即謂暉等：「吾受漢家厚恩，亡以報，今年老矣，旦暮入地，誼豈以一身事二姓，下見故主哉？」

translations.literal: Hui and others reported the envoy's words; Sheng knowing himself not to be heeded, then said to Hui and others: "I received the Han house's thick favor, have no means to repay; now year is old, morning and evening entering earth—how could principle take one body to serve two surnames, and below meet the former lord?"
translations.idiomatic: When his disciples relayed this, Gong Sheng knew they would not listen. He told them, "The Han treated me generously; I could never repay it. I am old and near the grave—how could I serve a second dynasty and face my late sovereign below?"

sentences.zh: 勝因敕以棺斂喪事：「衣周於身，棺周於衣。

translations.literal: Sheng thereupon ordered coffin and shroud funeral affairs: "Clothes sufficient for the body, coffin sufficient for the clothes.
translations.idiomatic: He then instructed them on his burial: "Let my clothes cover my limbs and my coffin cover my clothes—no more.

sentences.zh: 勿隨俗動吾冢，種柏，作祠堂。」

translations.literal: Do not follow vulgar custom moving my tomb, planting cypress, making a shrine hall."
translations.idiomatic: Do not follow fashion by heaping up my mound, planting cypress, or building a shrine."

sentences.zh: 語畢，遂不復開口飲食，積十四日死，死時七十九矣。

translations.literal: Words finished, then did not again open mouth to drink or eat; accumulated fourteen days died; at death was seventy-nine.
translations.idiomatic: He spoke no more, ate nothing, and died fourteen days later at seventy-nine.

sentences.zh: 使者、太守臨斂，賜複衾祭祠如法。

translations.literal: The envoy and governor attended the shrouding, bestowed double coverlet and sacrifice and offering according to law.
translations.idiomatic: The envoy and governor attended his laying-out and supplied the state shroud and rites as prescribed.

sentences.zh: 門人衰絰治喪者百數。

translations.literal: Disciples in mourning sash who managed the funeral were a hundred-odd.
translations.idiomatic: More than a hundred disciples wore mourning and handled the funeral.

sentences.zh: 有老父來弔，哭甚哀，既而曰：「嗟虖！

translations.literal: An old father came to condole, wept very sorrowfully, afterward said: "Alas!
translations.idiomatic: An old stranger came to mourn, wept bitterly, and cried, "Alas!

sentences.zh: 薰以香自燒，膏以明自銷。

translations.literal: Incense by fragrance burns itself; fat by brightness melts itself.
translations.idiomatic: Fragrant incense consumes itself; bright lamp-oil burns itself away.

sentences.zh: 龔生竟夭天年，非吾徒也。」

translations.literal: Master Gong in the end cut short heaven's years—not my follower."
translations.idiomatic: Master Gong has cut short the span Heaven gave him—he is no disciple of mine!"

sentences.zh: 遂趨而出，莫知其誰。

translations.literal: Then hurried and went out; none knew who he was.
translations.idiomatic: He hurried off; no one learned his name.

sentences.zh: 勝居彭城廉里，後世刻石表其里門。

translations.literal: Sheng dwelt in Lian hamlet of Pengcheng; later generations carved stone and marked his hamlet gate.
translations.idiomatic: Gong Sheng had lived in Lian Lane, Pengcheng; later ages carved a stone marker at his gate.

sentences.zh: 鮑宣字子都，渤海高城人也。

translations.literal: Bao Xuan, courtesy name Zidu, was a man of Gaocheng in Bohai.
translations.idiomatic: Bao Xuan, style Zidu, was a native of Gaocheng in Bohai commandery.

sentences.zh: 好學明經，為縣鄉嗇夫，守束州丞。

translations.literal: He loved learning and illumined the classics, became village sefu of the county, guarded aide of Shuzhou.
translations.idiomatic: He loved the classics and served as a village bailiff in his county, then as assistant magistrate in the Shu prefecture office.

sentences.zh: 後為都尉太守功曹，舉孝廉為郎，病去官，復為州從事。

translations.literal: Afterward became merit clerk to the commandant and governor, was recommended filial and incorrupt and became gentleman, by illness left office, again became staff aide to the province.
translations.idiomatic: He later was merit clerk to the commandant and governor, entered as a filial-and-incorrupt gentleman, retired ill, then joined a provincial staff.

sentences.zh: 大司馬衛將軍王商辟宣，薦為議郎，後以病去。

translations.literal: Grand Marshal and Guard General Wang Shang summoned Xuan, recommended him as consultant, afterward by illness departed.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Marshal Wang Shang engaged him as a consultant; he later resigned on health grounds.

sentences.zh: 哀帝初，大司空何武除宣為西曹掾，甚敬重焉，薦宣為諫大夫，遷豫州牧。

translations.literal: At the beginning of Emperor Ai, Grand Minister of Works He Wu appointed Xuan as Western Bureau clerk, greatly respected him, recommended Xuan as remonstrance grandee, transferred him to Governor of Yu Province.
translations.idiomatic: Early in Emperor Ai's reign He Wu named him a Western Bureau clerk, thought highly of him, recommended him as remonstrance grandee, then sent him out as governor of Yu Province.

sentences.zh: 歲餘，丞相司直郭欽奏「宣舉錯煩苛，代二千石署吏聽訟，所察過詔條。

translations.literal: After more than a year Director of Integrity for the Chancellor Guo Qin submitted: "Xuan's conduct of affairs is vexatious and harsh; replacing two-thousand-shi men he appoints clerks and hears lawsuits; what he investigates exceeds edict articles.
translations.idiomatic: A year later the chancellor's director of integrity Guo Qin charged that Bao Xuan ran his province with petty harshness, usurped a governor's authority to appoint clerks and hear cases, and exceeded the scope set by edict.

sentences.zh: 行部乘傳去法駕，駕一馬，舍宿鄉亭，為眾所非。」

translations.literal: When touring his department he rode relay post and cast off regulated equipage, drove one horse, lodged at village pavilions—was blamed by the multitude."
translations.idiomatic: On inspection tours he used a single horse, shed full escort, and slept at country post stations—the crowd thought him improper."

sentences.zh: 宣坐免。

translations.literal: Xuan sat and was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Bao Xuan was removed from office.

sentences.zh: 歸家數月，復徵為諫大夫。

translations.literal: Returned home several months, again summoned to be remonstrance grandee.
translations.idiomatic: After a few months at home he was recalled as remonstrance grandee.

sentences.zh: 宣每居位，常上書諫爭，其言少文多實。

translations.literal: Whenever Xuan dwelt in office, he constantly submitted memorials remonstrating and disputing; his words had little patterning and much substance.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever he held office he remonstrated incessantly—plain words, solid content.

sentences.zh: 是時帝祖母傅太后欲與成帝母俱稱尊號，封爵親屬，丞相孔光、大司空師丹、何武、大司馬傅喜始執正議，失傅太后指，皆免官。

translations.literal: At this time the emperor's grandmother the Grand Empress Dowager Fu wished together with Emperor Cheng's mother both to be called honored titles, enfeoff and noble kin; Chancellor Kong Guang, Grand Minister of Works Shi Dan, He Wu, Grand Marshal Fu Xi at first held correct discussion, missed the Grand Empress Dowager Fu's intent, all were removed from office.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor's grandmother, Grand Empress Dowager Fu, demanded equal honor with Emperor Cheng's mother and titles for her kin; Chancellor Kong Guang, Grand Ministers Shi Dan and He Wu, and Grand Marshal Fu Xi opposed her and were stripped of office.

sentences.zh: 丁、傅子弟並進，董賢貴幸，宣以諫大夫從其後，上書諫曰：

translations.literal: The Ding and Fu young men advanced together; Dong Xian was noble and favored; Xuan as remonstrance grandee followed after them, submitted a memorial remonstrating, saying:
translations.idiomatic: The Ding and Fu factions rose together with the favorite Dong Xian; Bao Xuan, again remonstrance grandee, sent a memorial that began:

sentences.zh: 竊見孝成皇帝時，外親持權，人人牽引所私以充塞朝廷，妨賢人路，濁亂天下，奢泰亡度，窮困百姓，是以日蝕且十，彗星四起。

translations.literal: Your servant privately sees that in the time of Emperor Cheng, outer kin grasped power; each person pulled in his private favorites to fill and block the court, obstructed the road of worthies, muddied and disordered all under Heaven, extravagance exceeded measure, exhausted and distressed the hundred surnames—therefore solar eclipses approached ten, comets arose in the four directions.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Cheng the maternal relatives seized power, packed the court with favorites, shut out good men, bankrupted the people, and brought on nearly ten eclipses and comets in every quarter.

sentences.zh: 危亡之徵，陛下所親見也，今柰何反覆劇於前乎！

translations.literal: The omens of peril and destruction Your Majesty personally saw; now how can it be that the reversal is worse than before!
translations.idiomatic: You saw those omens of ruin yourself—how can the relapse be worse than the first illness?

sentences.zh: 朝臣亡有大儒骨鯁，白首耆艾，魁壘之士；

translations.literal: Among court ministers there are none who are great Confucians with upright backbone, white-haired hoary, knightly and towering men;
translations.idiomatic: The court now boasts no great scholar with a straight spine, no white-haired pillar of integrity;

sentences.zh: 論議通古今，喟然動眾心，憂國如飢渴者，臣未見也。

translations.literal: in discussion comprehending antiquity and the present, sighingly moving the multitude's hearts, worrying for the state as hunger and thirst—your servant has not seen them.
translations.idiomatic: no one who can speak across past and present, stir the crowd, and thirst for the state's welfare as for food and drink.

sentences.zh: 敦外親小童及幸臣董賢等在公門省戶下，陛下欲與此共承天地，安海內，甚難。

translations.literal: Thickly the outer kin's young boys and the favored subject Dong Xian and others are beneath the public gate and inner doors; Your Majesty wishes with these together to receive Heaven and Earth and pacify within the seas—very difficult.
translations.idiomatic: Petty in-laws, boys, and the favorite Dong Xian cluster at the palace gates; to expect them to uphold Heaven and earth with you is hopeless.

sentences.zh: 今世俗謂不智者為能，謂智者為不能。

translations.literal: Now vulgar custom calls the not wise able, calls the wise unable.
translations.idiomatic: Custom now calls fools capable and calls the wise helpless.

sentences.zh: 昔堯放四罪而天下服，今除一吏而眾皆惑；

translations.literal: In antiquity Yao banished the four crimes and all under Heaven submitted; now removing one clerk and the multitude all are confused;
translations.idiomatic: Yao exiled four villains and the realm obeyed; you dismiss one clerk and everyone is bewildered;

sentences.zh: 古刑人尚服，今賞人反惑。

translations.literal: in antiquity punished men still submitted; now rewarding men on the contrary are confused.
translations.idiomatic: In antiquity even the punished submitted; today rewards only breed public confusion.

sentences.zh: 請寄為姦，群小日進。

translations.literal: Entrusting and lodging become treachery; the swarm of petty men daily advances.
translations.idiomatic: Backdoor dealing passes for duty; petty men advance daily.

sentences.zh: 國家空虛，用度不足。

translations.literal: The state is empty and void; expenses are not sufficient.
translations.idiomatic: The treasury is hollow and spending still outruns income.

sentences.zh: 民流亡，去城郭，盜賊並起，吏為殘賊，歲增於前。

translations.literal: The people flee and wander, leave city walls; bandits arise together; clerks act as cruel bandits, yearly increase compared to before.
translations.idiomatic: The people flee the towns, bandits multiply, and officials prey on them more viciously each year.

sentences.zh: 凡民有七亡：陰陽不和，水旱為災，一亡也；

translations.literal: In all the people have seven losses: yin and yang not harmonious, water and drought become disaster—the first loss;
translations.idiomatic: I count seven ways the people are ruined: first, when yin and yang fall out of tune and flood or drought strikes;

sentences.zh: 縣官重責更賦租稅，二亡也；

translations.literal: the county office heavily levies replacement fees, rent, and tax—the second loss;
translations.idiomatic: second, when the state piles on replacement levies, rent, and tax;

sentences.zh: 貪吏並公，受取不已，三亡也；

translations.literal: greedy clerks join the public, taking and receiving without end—the third loss;
translations.idiomatic: third, when corrupt clerks bleed the public without end;

sentences.zh: 豪強大姓蠶食亡厭，四亡也；

translations.literal: powerful magnates and great surnames silkworm-eat without satisfaction—the fourth loss;
translations.idiomatic: fourth, when great clans devour small holders insatiably;

sentences.zh: 苛吏繇役，失農桑時，五亡也；

translations.literal: harsh clerks and corvée labor miss the season of farming and mulberry—the fifth loss;
translations.idiomatic: fifth, when harsh corvée tears farmers from plow and loom at the wrong season;

sentences.zh: 部落鼓鳴，男女遮迣，六亡也；

translations.literal: village beats drum and sounds; men and women block and patrol—the sixth loss;
translations.idiomatic: sixth, when village alarms beat and men and women must turn out night watch;

sentences.zh: 盜賊劫略，取民財物，七亡也。

translations.literal: Bandits rob and plunder, taking the people's goods and wealth—the seventh loss.
translations.idiomatic: seventh, when bandits strip them of what little they own.

sentences.zh: 七亡尚可，又有七死：酷吏毆殺，一死也；

translations.literal: The seven losses are still bearable; again there are seven deaths: cruel clerks beat and kill—the first death;
translations.idiomatic: Seven kinds of ruin the people might survive—but there are also seven ways they die: first, when brutal officials beat them to death;

sentences.zh: 治獄深刻，二死也；

translations.literal: handling prisons deeply and cuttingly—the second death;
translations.idiomatic: second, when jailers torture them to death;

sentences.zh: 冤陷亡辜，三死也；

translations.literal: being wronged and trapped though without guilt—the third death;
translations.idiomatic: third, when the innocent are framed and perish;

sentences.zh: 盜賊橫發，四死也；

translations.literal: bandits erupting across—the fourth death;
translations.idiomatic: fourth, when bandits strike them down;

sentences.zh: 怨讎相殘，五死也；

translations.literal: enemies in mutual slaughter—the fifth death;
translations.idiomatic: fifth, when private feuds end in murder;

sentences.zh: 歲惡飢餓，六死也；

translations.literal: years evil and famine hunger—the sixth death;
translations.idiomatic: sixth, when bad years starve them;

sentences.zh: 時氣疾疫，七死也。

translations.literal: seasonal vapor sickness and pestilence—the seventh death.
translations.idiomatic: seventh, when plague carries them off.

sentences.zh: 民有七亡而無一得，欲望國安，誠難；

translations.literal: The people have seven losses yet not one gain—to desire the state secure is truly difficult;
translations.idiomatic: Seven losses against no gain—how can the state stand firm?

sentences.zh: 民有七死而無一生，欲望刑措，誠難。

translations.literal: the people have seven deaths yet not one life—to desire punishments set aside is truly difficult.
translations.idiomatic: Seven deaths and no reprieve—how can you expect the rack to stay idle?

sentences.zh: 此非公卿守相貪殘成化之所致邪？

translations.literal: Is this not what the dukes, ministers, governors, and chancellors by greed and cruelty completing transformation have brought about?
translations.idiomatic: Is this not the work of greedy, cruel ministers and local rulers?

sentences.zh: 群臣幸得居尊官，食重祿，豈有肯加惻隱於細民，助陛下流教化者邪？

translations.literal: The host of ministers fortunately obtain to dwell in honored offices, eating heavy salary—how could there be those willing to add pity on the slender people, assisting Your Majesty to flow transformation?
translations.idiomatic: You give your high ministers fat salaries—yet who spares a thought for the poor or helps you teach the realm?

sentences.zh: 志但在營私家，稱賓客，為姦利而已。

translations.literal: Their intent is only to manage private households, praise retainers, and act for treacherous profit—that is all.
translations.idiomatic: They scheme only for private gain, flatter clients, and trade in influence.

sentences.zh: 以苟容曲從為賢，以拱默尸祿為智，謂如臣宣等為愚。

translations.literal: They take compliant accommodation and crooked following as worthy, take folded silence and occupying salary as wisdom, and call men like your servant Xuan foolish.
translations.idiomatic: They call toadying "worth," silence at court "wisdom," and men like me fools.

sentences.zh: 陛下擢臣巖穴，誠冀有益豪毛，豈徒欲使臣美食大官，重高門之地哉！

translations.literal: Your Majesty pulled up your servant from cliff and cave, truly hoping there might be benefit the size of a fine hair—could it be only wishing to make your servant eat fine food at the Grand Provisioner and weigh heavy the gate of high doors!
translations.idiomatic: You raised me from obscurity hoping for some small use—not merely to fatten me at the imperial kitchen and puff up my gate!

sentences.zh: 天下乃皇天之天下也，陛下上為皇天子，下為黎庶父母，為天牧養元元，視之當如一，合尸鳩之詩。

translations.literal: All under Heaven is then August Heaven's all under Heaven; Your Majesty above is August Heaven's Son, below is father and mother to black-haired commoners; for Heaven you shepherd and nurture the multitude; in viewing them ought to be as one, matching the ode on the cuckoo.
translations.idiomatic: The empire belongs to Heaven; you are its Son above and parent to the people below, charged to nurture every life alike—as the Odes ask of the fair cuckoo feeding its young.

sentences.zh: 今貧民菜食不厭，衣又穿空，父子夫婦不能相保，誠可為酸鼻。

translations.literal: Now the poor people with vegetable food are not satisfied, clothes again pierced through with holes; father and son, husband and wife cannot preserve each other—truly can be called nose-stinging with acid.
translations.idiomatic: The poor choke down greens without filling their bellies and wear rags that will not hold a family together—a sight to wring the heart.

sentences.zh: 陛下不救，將安所歸命乎？

translations.literal: If Your Majesty does not save them, where will they turn to consign their lives?
translations.idiomatic: If you will not save them, where can they look for life?

sentences.zh: 奈何獨私養外親與幸臣董賢，多賞賜以大萬數，使奴從賓客漿酒霍肉，蒼頭廬兒皆用致富！

translations.literal: Why alone privately nurture outer kin and the favored subject Dong Xian, many bestowals reaching tens of thousands in large numbers, causing slaves to follow retainers pouring wine and splashing meat, blue-head hut lads all thereby becoming rich!
translations.idiomatic: Yet you lavish tens of thousands on in-laws and on Dong Xian, while their slaves and guests pour wine and waste meat until every groom in the stables grows rich.

sentences.zh: 非天意也。

translations.literal: It is not Heaven's intention.
translations.idiomatic: That cannot be Heaven's will.

sentences.zh: 及汝昌侯傅商亡功而封。

translations.literal: Reaching Marquis of Ruchang Fu Shang without merit yet enfeoffed.
translations.idiomatic: Fu Shang of Ruchang was made a marquis without a shred of merit.

sentences.zh: 夫官爵非陛下之官爵，乃天下之官爵也。

translations.literal: As to office and noble rank, they are not Your Majesty's office and noble rank—they are all under Heaven's office and noble rank.
translations.idiomatic: Rank and fief belong to the realm, not to the throne alone.

sentences.zh: 陛下取非其官，官非其人，而望天說民服，豈不難哉！

translations.literal: Your Majesty takes what is not its office, offices what is not its person, yet hopes Heaven is pleased and the people submit—is it not difficult!
translations.idiomatic: You hand out titles to the wrong men for the wrong reasons and still expect Heaven to smile and the people to bow—how can that be?

sentences.zh: 方陽侯孫寵、宜陵侯息夫躬辯足以移眾，彊可用獨立，姦人之雄，或世尤劇者也，宜以時罷退。

translations.literal: Marquis of Fangyang Sun Chong and Marquis of Yiling Xifu Gong have eloquence enough to shift the multitude, strength enough to stand alone—champions among knaves, perhaps the worst in a generation—it is fitting in due time to dismiss and retire them.
translations.idiomatic: Sun Chong of Fangyang and Xifu Gong of Yiling wield tongues that sway crowds and wills that bow to no one—ringleaders of villainy among the worst of the age—and should be cashiered at once.

sentences.zh: 及外親幼童未通經術者，皆宜令休就師傅。

translations.literal: Reaching outer-kin young boys who have not yet penetrated classic arts, all it is fitting to order them to rest and go to masters.
translations.idiomatic: Pack the young in-laws who know no classics off to tutors.

sentences.zh: 急徵故大司馬傅喜使領外親。

translations.literal: Urgently summon the former Grand Marshal Fu Xi and order him to oversee the outer kin.
translations.idiomatic: Recall Fu Xi, the former grand marshal, to keep watch on the affines.

sentences.zh: 故大司空何武、師丹、故丞相孔光、故左將軍彭宣，經皆更博士，位皆歷三公，智謀威信，可與建教化，圖安危。

translations.literal: Former Grand Minister of Works He Wu, Shi Dan, former Chancellor Kong Guang, former Left General Peng Xuan—their classics all were changed to erudite; their positions all passed through the Three Dukes; wisdom, planning, prestige, and trustworthiness can with them establish transformation and plan peril and safety.
translations.idiomatic: He Wu, Shi Dan, Kong Guang, and Peng Xuan—each a former minister of the highest rank, each a master of the classics—have the wisdom and prestige to restore moral government and steady the state.

sentences.zh: 龔勝為司直，郡國皆慎選舉，三輔委輸官不敢為姦，可大委任也。

translations.literal: Gong Sheng as Director of Integrity—commanderies and states all carefully selected recommendations; the Three Adjacencies transport officials did not dare act treacherously—can be greatly entrusted.
translations.idiomatic: When Gong Sheng was director of integrity, local elections were honest and the capital transport clerks dared not cheat—such men deserve your full trust.

sentences.zh: 陛下前以小不忍退武等，海內失望。

translations.literal: Your Majesty before by small lack of forbearance dismissed Wu and others—within the seas disappointment.
translations.idiomatic: You dismissed He Wu and his like for a trifle—and the empire lost heart.

sentences.zh: 陛下尚能容亡功德者甚眾，曾不能忍武等邪！

translations.literal: Your Majesty still is able to tolerate those without merit and virtue in very great number—could you not forbear with Wu and others!
translations.idiomatic: You tolerate countless mediocrities—could you not spare these few good men?

sentences.zh: 治天下者當用天下之心為心，不得自專快意而已也。

translations.literal: Those who order all under Heaven ought to use all under Heaven's hearts as heart; cannot obtain self alone following pleasure and intent—that is all.
translations.idiomatic: A ruler must think with the mind of the empire, not indulge private whim.

sentences.zh: 上之皇天見譴，下之黎庶怨恨，次有諫爭之臣，陛下苟欲自薄而厚惡臣，天下猶不聽也。

translations.literal: Above August Heaven sees reproach; below the black-haired harbor resentment; next there are ministers who remonstrate and dispute—if Your Majesty wishes to slight yourself and thickly load evil on ministers, all under Heaven still will not listen.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven frowns above, the people mutter below, and we remonstrators still speak—yet if you mean to ruin yourself and blame your ministers, the realm will not follow you there either.

sentences.zh: 臣雖愚戇，獨不知多受祿賜，美食太官，廣田宅，厚妻子，不與惡人結讎怨以安身邪？

translations.literal: Your servant although foolish and blunt, alone does not know that receiving many salaries and bestowals, eating fine food at the Grand Provisioner, broadening fields and dwellings, thickening wife and children, not joining with evil men to knot enmity and resentment thereby to secure the body?
translations.idiomatic: Do you think I do not know I could take your silks, feast at your kitchen, enlarge my estate, enrich my family, and keep silent to avoid enemies?

sentences.zh: 誠迫大義，官以諫爭為職，不敢不竭愚。

translations.literal: Truly pressed by great right; the office takes remonstrance and dispute as duty—does not dare not exhaust foolishness.
translations.idiomatic: But my office is remonstrance, and I dare not hold back.

sentences.zh: 惟陛下少留神明，覽五經之文，原聖人之至意，深思天地之戒。

translations.literal: Only may Your Majesty slightly retain numinous clarity, peruse the texts of the Five Classics, trace the utmost intent of the sages, deeply ponder Heaven and Earth's warnings.
translations.idiomatic: I beg you to pause, read the Five Classics, recover the sages' deepest intent, and heed the warnings of Heaven and earth.

sentences.zh: 臣宣吶鈍於辭，不勝惓惓，盡死節而已。

translations.literal: Your servant Xuan is dull and blunt in phrases, cannot overcome earnest loyalty—only exhaust death in integrity—that is all.
translations.idiomatic: I am clumsy with words but loyal to the bone—I can offer you nothing less than my life.

sentences.zh: 上以宣名儒，優容之。

translations.literal: The emperor because Xuan was a famed Confucian treated him with indulgence.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor, knowing Bao Xuan a noted scholar, bore with him.

sentences.zh: 是時郡國地震，民訛言行籌，明年正月朔日蝕，上乃徵孔光，免孫寵、息夫躬，罷侍中諸曹黃門郎數十人。

translations.literal: At this time commanderies and states had earthquakes; the people had false words and acted counting-sticks; the next year first month new moon had solar eclipse; the emperor then summoned Kong Guang, dismissed Sun Chong and Xifu Gong, dismissed several tens of attendants-in-ordinary and various bureau yellow-gate gentlemen.
translations.idiomatic: Earthquakes shook the commanderies; rumors and divination panics spread; a new-year eclipse followed. The emperor recalled Kong Guang, removed Sun Chong and Xifu Gong, and purged dozens of palace gentlemen.

sentences.zh: 宣復上書言：

translations.literal: Xuan again submitted a memorial saying:
translations.idiomatic: Bao Xuan memorialized again:

sentences.zh: 陛下父事天，母事地，子養黎民，即位已來，父虧明，母震動，子訛言相驚恐。

translations.literal: Your Majesty serves Heaven as father, serves Earth as mother, rears the black-haired as children; since ascending the throne, the father has lacked brightness, the mother has quaked, the children have false words and mutually frightened each other.
translations.idiomatic: You serve Heaven as a father and Earth as a mother and the people as your children—yet since your accession Heaven has dimmed, Earth has trembled, and the people have spread rumors of terror.

sentences.zh: 今日蝕於三始，誠可畏懼。

translations.literal: Today eclipse is at the three beginnings—truly to be feared and dreaded.
translations.idiomatic: Today's eclipse falls on the "three beginnings" of the year—a terrifying omen.

sentences.zh: 小民正月朔日尚恐毀敗器物，何況於日虧乎！

translations.literal: Petty people on the first day of the first month still fear destroying and breaking utensils—how much more on the sun's being diminished!
translations.idiomatic: Even commoners fear breaking dishes on New Year's Day—how much more should we fear an eclipse of the sun!

sentences.zh: 陛下深內自責，避正殿，舉直言，求過失，罷退外親及旁仄素餐之人，徵拜孔光為光祿大夫，發覺孫寵、息夫躬過惡，免官遣就國，眾庶歙然，莫不說喜。

translations.literal: Your Majesty deeply inward blamed yourself, avoided the main hall, raised straight words, sought faults and oversights, dismissed and retired outer kin and those beside and lateral who eat plain food, summoned and appointed Kong Guang as Grand Counselor of the Household, exposed Sun Chong and Xifu Gong's faults and evils, removed office and sent them to their states—the multitude suddenly, none not pleased and joyful.
translations.idiomatic: You withdrew to self-blame, left the main hall, called for honest counsel, cashiered useless kin and hangers-on, named Kong Guang grand counselor of the household, stripped Sun Chong and Xifu Gong of office and sent them to their fiefs—and the people drew a breath of joy.

sentences.zh: 天人同心，人心說則天意解矣。

translations.literal: Heaven and man share one heart; when the people's hearts are pleased then Heaven's intent is released.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven and the people move as one; when hearts ease, Heaven's anger lifts.

sentences.zh: 乃二月丙戌，白虹虷日，連陰不雨，此天有憂結未解，民有怨望未塞者也。

translations.literal: Then on second month bingxu, white rainbow pierced the sun, continuous yin without rain—this is Heaven having anxious knots not yet released, the people having resentful gaze not yet blocked.
translations.idiomatic: Yet on bingxu in the second month a white rainbow cut the sun, clouds hung unbroken, and no rain fell—Heaven's grief is not yet spent, nor the people's resentment slaked.

sentences.zh: 侍中駙馬都尉董賢本無葭莩之親，但以令色諛言自進，賞賜亡度，竭盡府藏，并合三第尚以為小，復壞暴室。

translations.literal: Attendant-in-ordinary and Commandant of Cavalry Dong Xian originally had no reed-and-rush kinship; but by pleasing looks and flattering words advanced himself; bestowals without limit, exhausted the treasury storehouses; merging three mansions still thought it small, again destroyed the Exposing Room.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Xian, attendant-in-ordinary and commandant of cavalry, is no kin of yours; he rose on a pretty face and smooth words; his gifts have drained the treasury; three mansions merged into one palace were still too small, so he tore down the imperial wardrobe hall besides.

sentences.zh: 賢父子坐使天子使者將作治第，行夜吏卒皆得賞賜。

translations.literal: Xian father and son sit and cause the Son of Heaven's envoys and chief of works to build the mansion; night-patrol clerks and soldiers all obtain bestowals.
translations.idiomatic: Father and son sit at ease while imperial envoys and the chief of works build their house, and every night watchman goes home with a tip.

sentences.zh: 上冢有會，輒太官為供。

translations.literal: When visiting tombs there is a gathering, then the Grand Provisioner supplies.
translations.idiomatic: When they visit the family graves the imperial kitchen caters the feast.

sentences.zh: 海內貢獻當養一君，今反盡之賢家，豈天意與民意邪！
